name: Laos
tagline: Landlocked serenity of temples and rivers

scenarios.description: Immigration and customs at Lao airports

arrivalSteps.title: Visa Arrangements (e-Visa or VOA)
arrivalSteps.description: Most nationalities can apply for a Laos e-Visa (30-day single entry) at laoevisa.gov.la at least 3 days before arrival. Visa on Arrival (USD 30–42 depending on nationality) is available at major airports (Wattay/Vientiane, Luang Prabang). ASEAN citizens receive visa-free entry.

arrivalSteps.title: Arrival Card Completion
arrivalSteps.description: Fill in the Lao Arrival/Departure card distributed on the aircraft or available at the immigration hall. Provide your name, passport number, nationality, flight number, occupation, and Lao accommodation address.

arrivalSteps.title: Health Declaration
arrivalSteps.description: A health declaration form may be required at certain entry points depending on current disease alerts. Yellow fever vaccination proof is required if arriving from a yellow fever endemic country.

arrivalSteps.title: Passport Control / Immigration
arrivalSteps.description: Present your passport, completed arrival card, and e-Visa or VOA receipt at the immigration counter. Officers will stamp your passport. Ensure your passport has at least 6 months validity and a blank page for the stamp.

arrivalSteps.title: Baggage Claim
arrivalSteps.description: Collect your checked luggage from the carousel. Laos airports are small; the process is generally quick but baggage may be manually screened by customs before exit.

arrivalSteps.title: Customs Declaration
arrivalSteps.description: Duty-free: 200 cigarettes or 500g tobacco, 1 bottle of spirits, and personal effects of reasonable quantity. Declare cash over USD 2,000. Prohibited: narcotics (death penalty), antiques and Buddha images (require import/export permits), and wildlife products.

fallbackAlerts.message: Drug possession or trafficking in Laos carries lengthy imprisonment or the death penalty.
Bring USD or THB to exchange. ATMs are limited outside Vientiane and Luang Prabang.

phrases.nativeScript: ຮ້ານແລກເງິນຢູ່ໃສ?
ຂ້ອຍຕ້ອງການແທ໋ກຊີ.

scenarios.description: Buddhist temple customs and monk interaction rules

arrivalSteps.title: Dress Modestly
arrivalSteps.description: Wear clothing that covers shoulders and knees. Temples in Luang Prabang are strict about this.

arrivalSteps.title: Monk Photography Etiquette
arrivalSteps.description: Always ask permission before photographing monks. Women must never touch or hand items directly to a monk.

arrivalSteps.title: Morning Alms Ceremony
arrivalSteps.description: The tak bat (alms-giving) in Luang Prabang is sacred. Observe from a respectful distance. Do not intrude or use flash.

fallbackAlerts.message: The morning alms procession (tak bat) is a sacred ceremony. Stand back, keep quiet, and absolutely no flash photography.
Remove shoes before entering any temple hall or sacred area.

phrases.nativeScript: ຂ້ອຍສາມາດເຂົ້າໄດ້ບໍ?

phrases.english: Where should I remove my shoes?
phrases.nativeScript: ຂ້ອຍຄວນຖອດເກີບທີ່ໃດ?
phrases.romanized: Khoi khuan thot keub thi dai?

scenarios.description: Handicraft shopping and night market etiquette

arrivalSteps.title: Light Bargaining Is Acceptable
arrivalSteps.description: Gentle bargaining is fine, but Lao vendors are less aggressive than in neighbouring countries. A smile goes far.

fallbackAlerts.message: Exporting antiques and Buddha images from Laos is strictly controlled. Check with customs before purchasing old items.

phrases.nativeScript: ອັນນີ້ລາຄາເທົ່າໃດ?
